source.name: gstack by Garry Tan
source.license_notes: MIT — patterns extracted and generalized, not copied verbatim

goal: Establish a Think→Plan→Build→Review→Test→Ship→Reflect 7-stage software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development, replacing single-prompt code generation with disciplined engineering workflow.
when_to_use: Starting a new feature or project
steps: Think: redefine the problem, answer 6 mandatory questions
Plan: design architecture, data flow, test matrix; pass required review gates
Build: implement per plan, write tests, checkpoint commits
Review: code review, design audit, DX audit, security audit (as applicable)
Test: browser QA, regression tests, security scan
Reflect: write retro, update rules/checklists/templates in database
prompt_contract: Agent must not skip from Think to Build without Plan
Each stage has a stop condition that must be met
Output of each stage feeds the next stage
User has final say over AI recommendations (User Sovereignty)
verification: All review gates passed before proceeding
failure_modes: Agent jumps directly to Build without Think/Plan
Skipping tests because it seems to work
Ignoring review gate failures to proceed faster
handoff_report: Report current stage and completion status
List any failed review gates and remaining issues

quality.notes: Pattern extracted from gstack v1.21.1.0, generalized for any AI agent platform. The 7-stage flow is battle-tested in gstack's own development and applicable to any software project.

retrieval.ai_summary: Think-Plan-Build-Review-Test-Ship-Reflect 7-stage agentic software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development. Agent must go through all stages instead of jumping directly to code. Each stage has stop conditions and review gates.
prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect
